toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run tests with blame-crash to collect crash dumps

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run tests with blame-crash to collect crash dumps

data.reasoningText:
This is a .NET 9 MSTest project. The user wants to run the tests and collect a crash dump if any test crashes the host process. 

For .NET, the way to collect crash dumps on test host crashes is to use environment variables:
- `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1` — enables mini dump collection on crash
- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4` — full dump (MiniDumpWithFullMemory)
- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName=<path>` — where to write the dump

Alternatively, you can use `dotnet test` with the `--blame-crash` option, which is specifically designed to collect crash dumps when the test host crashes. This is the idiomatic way.


I'll run the tests with the `--blame-crash` flag and specify a full dump type to capture complete memory information if the test host crashes.

arguments.description: Run tests with blame-crash to collect crash dumps

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-3961ffc11ff64e48a1a0a6cc5807f0a9 && dotnet test --blame-crash 2>&1", "description": "Run tests with blame-crash to collect crash dumps", "initial_wait": 60}

data.content:
All 3 tests passed. Here's a summary:

**Command used:**
```
dotnet test --blame-crash
```

**Results:** ✅ All tests succeeded (build + test in ~22s)

The `--blame-crash` flag enables the blame data collector, which will automatically capture a crash dump (via `procdump`/`createdump`) if the test host process crashes unexpectedly. No crashes occurred during this run, so no dump was generated.
